plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and comply with the single name mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL holdings to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, aligning with capital preservation.
Trim QQQI to 15% as it was flagged for trimming, while maintaining income exposure.
Buy XLV (Healthcare) at 8% to diversify the portfolio away from tech concentration.
Buy RTX (Industrials) at 7% to further diversify and add a top-quality name.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation, reduces leverage, and diversifies holdings.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets around 1.5% per month, focusing on capital preservation and reduced drawdown. It significantly lowers leverage and concentration, but potential upside is limited.

moves.note: Reduce leveraged ETF exposure to small

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to reduce concentration and adhere to the single name mandate.
Sell SOXL entirely due to its significant loss and below-stop status.
Trim SPAL to 2% to reduce overall leveraged ETF exposure to a small allocation.
Trim QQQI to 15% as it was flagged, while still retaining some income exposure.
Buy CRDO at 8% and ANET at 7% to add high-scoring tech setups, balancing growth with risk.
This strategy aims for risk-first growth by reducing leverage and diversifying into quality tech.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets around 2.5% per month, aiming for risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration, offering a moderate risk/reward profile with controlled drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to comply with the concentration mandate, freeing capital for high-conviction plays.
Sell SOXL entirely due to its significant underperformance and below-stop status; reallocate.
Trim QQQI to 10% to reduce income focus and increase allocation to growth opportunities.
Buy CRDO at 10% and ANET at 10% to concentrate in top-scoring, high-potential tech setups.
Add to SMH, increasing its allocation to 18%, leveraging its strong scanner read and sector exposure.
This plan leans into high-scoring setups and allows for increased concentration within limits for aggressive growth.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% per month, aiming for stretch growth. It embraces higher concentration and leverage in top setups, but comes with significantly increased drawdown risk and potential for rapid losses, especially with leveraged ETFs.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and reallocate capital towards income-generating assets.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL holdings to eliminate leveraged ETFs and reduce high-beta exposure.
Sell SMH and CRWD to further reduce high-beta tech exposure, prioritizing stability.
Add to QQQI, increasing its allocation to 30%, to enhance steady monthly distributions.
This plan focuses on rotating into income and broad index ETFs for reliable distributions.
The goal is to prioritize low drawdown and consistent income over aggressive growth.
income.read: This income plan targets around 1% per month, prioritizing steady distributions and low drawdown. While aiming for consistent income, distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in selloffs. This is not a savings account.
